title: 7 Herbal Teas For Motion Sickness
intro_desc: If you're prone to motion sickness, you might want to try drinking herbal teas to help ease your symptoms. These teas work by helping to calm your stomach and reduce nausea, which makes it easier to travel by car, boat, or plane. Some herbs that are known to have this effect include ginger (Zingiber officinale), ginkgo biloba, and valerian root (Valeriana officinalis).  Ginger is a natural anti-inflammatory that can help to soothe your stomach and reduce nausea. It's often used to treat digestive issues, and it's also a natural pain reliever. Ginkgo biloba, on the other hand, is an herb that's known to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation. This can help to ease the symptoms of motion sickness, which are often caused by poor blood flow to the brain.  Valerian root is another herb that's often used to treat motion sickness. It's a natural relaxant that can help to calm your stomach and reduce anxiety, which are both common causes of motion sickness. Drinking valerian root tea before traveling can help to ease your symptoms and make your journey more comfortable.  Drinking herbal teas for motion sickness can bring many benefits to your life. For one, it can help to reduce your anxiety and stress levels, which can make traveling more enjoyable. It can also help to prevent motion sickness from occurring in the first place, which means you can travel more freely without worrying about getting sick. Additionally, herbal teas are a natural and chemical-free way to treat motion sickness, which can be especially beneficial for people who are sensitive to medications.
faq_1: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent motion sickness. Peppermint and ginger teas are often recommended because they can calm your stomach and reduce nausea. Some people find that sipping on these teas before traveling or during a boat ride helps them feel better. This may be due to their soothing effects on the digestive system.
faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent motion sickness. Peppermint tea, in particular, is known to calm the stomach and reduce nausea. Ginger tea is also a good option, as ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the digestive system. These teas can be a gentle way to ease motion sickness symptoms.
faq_safe: It's generally okay to drink herbal teas for motion sickness every day, but check the ingredients. Some teas have ingredients that can interact with medications or worsen health issues if consumed regularly. Peppermint, ginger, and lemon balm teas are often considered safe, but it's a good idea to read labels carefully and be aware of potential effects on your body.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas like ginger and peppermint can help with motion sickness. The results can start to show after 15-30 minutes of drinking the tea. Some people may feel better within 30 minutes, while others may need an hour or two to notice an improvement. It may take a few tries to figure out what works best for you.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for motion sickness can be most effective when you're feeling queasy, which is usually during the ride or after getting in the car. This is often when you're most likely to feel symptoms like dizziness and nausea. Drinking the tea at this time might help calm your stomach and reduce your discomfort.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas like ginger and peppermint can help with motion sickness, but they might interact with prescription medications. If you're taking meds for motion sickness, check the label for interactions with herbs. Some herbal teas can weaken the effect of prescription meds or change how they work in the body, so it's best to be cautious.
faq_pregnant: If you're pregnant and experiencing motion sickness, some herbal teas might help. Peppermint and ginger teas are often used to ease nausea. However, it's essential to check the ingredients and ensure they're safe during pregnancy. Some herbal teas can stimulate the uterus, which isn't recommended during pregnancy. Choose teas that are specifically labeled as "pregnancy-safe."
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for motion sickness are often made with peppermint, ginger, or other herbs. These teas can be safe to consume while breastfeeding, but it's best to check the ingredients and look for products labeled as "safe for breastfeeding." Some herbs, like sage and pennyroyal, have been linked to issues with milk supply or baby health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas contains a compound called gingerol, which is a bioactive constituent that has properties that help with motion sickness. Gingerol has anti-inflammatory properties that can ease stomach discomfort and nausea, which are common symptoms of motion sickness. It also has anticholinergic properties, which can help slow down the movement of stomach acid and reduce the risk of vomiting. Additionally, gingerol has antihistamine properties that can help relax the stomach muscles and reduce nausea. Drinking Zingiber officinale tea before traveling can help alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness by reducing nausea and vomiting.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root, 1 cup of boiling water, and a strainer or tea infuser.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root into a cup or tea infuser.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Zingiber officinale root.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into another cup.
Drink the tea as needed, ideally 30 minutes before traveling to help prevent motion sickness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas contains flavonoids and bilobalide, two active constituents that help alleviate motion sickness. The flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, have antioxidant properties that reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. Bilobalide, on the other hand, has a relaxing effect on the blood vessels, which can help to reduce the symptoms of motion sickness by improving blood flow to the inner ear. This improved blood flow helps to stabilize balance and reduce the body's reaction to motion. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy blood flow, ginkgo biloba teas can help to alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal teas for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and put them in the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the infuser or strainer.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser or strainer.
Drink the Ginkgo biloba tea and consider repeating every 4-6 hours as needed to alleviate motion sickness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas contains valerenic acid, isovalerene, and valepotriates as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have sedative and antispasmodic properties that help to relax the stomach muscles and reduce nausea. The antihistamine properties of valerenic acid also help to prevent the release of histamine, a chemical that can cause dizziness and nausea. By reducing inflammation and calming the stomach, valeriana officinalis teas may provide relief from motion sickness symptoms. The bioactive compounds in valeriana officinalis teas work together to help alleviate the discomfort and nausea associated with motion sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal teas for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Valeriana officinalis root and a cup of boiling water.
Pour the boiling water over the dried root in a cup and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into another cup to remove the root. Discard the root.
Add honey to taste, if needed, to sweeten the tea.
Drink the tea 30 minutes before traveling to help prevent motion sickness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata teas contains several bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides. The flavonoids in Passiflora incarnata, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and calm the nervous system. The alkaloids, particularly harmine and harmaline, interact with serotonin receptors in the brain, which can help stabilize and balance the body's natural response to motion. The glycosides in Passiflora incarnata, like passiflorine, also contribute to its sedative effects, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. By reducing inflammation, calming the nervous system, and balancing serotonin levels, Passiflora incarnata teas may help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal teas for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Passiflora incarnata leaves. Use 1/2 cup for a light tea and 1 cup for a stronger tea.
Combine the leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea with a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired. You can also add ice for a chilled tea.
Drink the tea 30 minutes to 1 hour before traveling to help prevent motion sickness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas contains a group of active compounds called alkaloids, particularly quinine and quinidine, which have been used to help alleviate motion sickness. These alkaloids have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can help reduce the symptoms of motion sickness. Quinine, in particular, has been shown to have a sedative effect on the central nervous system, which can help calm the digestive system and reduce nausea. Quinidine also has a natural anticholinergic effect, which can help stabilize the stomach and prevent vomiting. By consuming Cinchona officinalis teas, individuals may experience relief from the discomfort and nausea associated with motion sickness due to these alkaloids' properties.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal teas for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of Cinchona officinalis bark and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Cinchona bark in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and stir well.
Drink the tea 30 minutes before traveling to help alleviate motion sickness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare teas contains the bioactive constituents anethole and fenchone, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These properties help to calm the digestive system and relieve nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness. Anethole has a sedative effect on the nervous system, reducing the symptoms of motion sickness, such as dizziness and lightheadedness. Fenchone helps to relax the smooth muscle in the digestive tract, reducing the spasms and cramps that often occur during motion sickness. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the digestive system, Foeniculum vulgare teas can help alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal teas for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1/4 cup of dried Foeniculum vulgare seeds (also known as aniseed) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to a cup.
Pour 1/2 cup of the boiling water over the honey in the cup.
Add the dried Foeniculum vulgare seeds to the cup and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it slowly before and during travel to help prevent motion sickness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum teas contains a compound called Eugenol, which has anti-inflammatory properties. It also contains Cinnamaldehyde, a compound that helps to stabilize blood flow and improve circulation. These properties can help to alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the stomach. Cinnamomum verum teas also contains limonene, a compound that has been shown to have a calming effect on the digestive system. By reducing inflammation and improving circulation, Cinnamomum verum teas can help to provide relief from the symptoms of motion sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal teas for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Take 1 teaspoon of Cinnamomum verum bark and add it to a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth.
Pour the boiled water over the Cinnamomum verum bark in the tea infuser or cheesecloth.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then remove the tea infuser or cheesecloth.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, as needed for motion sickness relief.
